name: USS Melvin R. Nawman (DE 416)
events: 29 Jul 1945 At 1430 I/29, north-east of Chichijima, Bonin Islands, in position 28°40'N, 139°40'E, USS Seahorse (T/Cdr. H.H. Greer, Jr., USN) sighted six ships on the horizon with high periscope on bearing 058°.

At 1445 I/29, identities were exchanged with the escort carrier USS Anzio (T/Capt. G.C. Montgomery, USN). She was in the area for A/S patrol (Task Group 30.6) and was en-route to a refueling rendezvous with her screen which was made up of the destroyer escorts USS Lawrence C. Taylor (T/Lt.Cdr. J.R. Grey, USN, with COMCORTDIV 72, Cdr. A. Jackson, Jr. USNR on board), USS Melvin R. Nawman (T/Lt.Cdr. F.W. Kinsley, USN), USS Oliver Mitchell (Cdr. K.J. Barclay, USNR), USS Tabberer (Lt.Cdr. D.A. Decoudres, USNR) and USS Robert F. Keller (Cdr. R.J. Toner, USNR). (1)
